# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based frontend application for human resources management, built with TypeScript and powered by Supabase as the backend. The application follows a component-driven architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Tech Stack
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build System**: Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui component library
- **Routing**: React Router for client-side navigation
- **State Management**: React Query (TanStack Query) for server state management
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Edge Functions)
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit testing, Playwright for E2E testing

## Key Components and Architecture

### Layout System
- `AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout with sidebar navigation
- `AppSidebar.tsx`: Collapsible sidebar with navigation menu
- `TopBar.tsx`: Top navigation bar with user menu and notifications
- `NotificationBell.tsx`: Notification dropdown component
- `UserMenu.tsx`: User profile and logout functionality

### Core Features

#### Employee Management
- `SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Reusable component for selecting employees with search and avatar display
- Role-based access control with different permission levels (CEO, HR Manager, Team Lead, Employee)

#### Evaluations System
- `EvaluationTimeline.tsx`: Displays both quarterly and daily evaluations in a unified timeline
- Supports bi-annual performance reviews and daily feedback
- Role-based visibility controls for sensitive evaluation data

#### Financial Dashboard
- `FinanceSummary.tsx`: Comprehensive financial overview with payroll, expenses, and trend analysis
- 6-month trend visualization using Recharts
- Month-over-month comparison with percentage changes

#### Leave Management
- `ApplyLeaveModal.tsx`: Modal for submitting leave requests
- `LeaveBalancesTab.tsx`: Display current leave balances
- `AllRequestsTab.tsx`: Admin view of all leave requests
- `MyRequestsTab.tsx`: Employee view of their own requests

#### Payroll System
- `PayrollSummary.tsx`: Payroll overview and calculations
- Automated overtime and bonus calculations

#### Settings and Administration
- Multiple settings tabs for company configuration:
  - Departments, Roles, Leave Types
  - Attendance settings, Expense categories
  - Evaluation parameters, Notifications

### UI Component Library
The application uses shadcn/ui components extensively, including:
- Form controls (Button, Input, Select, etc.)
- Data display (Table, Card, Badge)
- Navigation (Tabs, Breadcrumb)
- Feedback (Toast, Alert, Dialog)
- Layout (Accordion, Collapsible, Resizable)

## Database Schema and Relationships

### Core Entities
- **companies**: Company information
- **employees**: Employee profiles with role assignments
- **departments**: Organizational structure
- **evaluations**: Bi-annual performance reviews
- **daily_evaluations**: Daily feedback system
- **leave_requests**: Leave management
- **payroll_records**: Payroll data
- **monthly_expenses**: Financial tracking

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies and departments
- Evaluations link employees to evaluators
- Leave requests have approval workflows
- Payroll records track monthly payments with OT and bonuses

## Business Logic and Data Flow

### Authentication and Authorization
- Supabase Auth handles user authentication
- Role-based permissions control feature access
- JWT tokens manage session state

### Data Fetching Strategy
- React Query manages server state with caching
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Background refetching for data freshness

### Notification System
- Automated notifications for HR events
- Role-based notification targeting
- Integration with Supabase Edge Functions

## Development and Deployment

### Development Workflow
- Hot module replacement with Vite
- TypeScript for type safety
- ESLint for code quality
- Prettier for code formatting

### Build and Deployment
- Optimized production builds with Vite
- Static asset optimization
- Environment-based configuration
- Supabase integration for backend services

## Key Files and Entry Points

- `src/main.tsx`: Application entry point
- `src/App.tsx`: Root component with routing
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Main dashboard page
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Utility functions
- `src/integrations/supabase/`: Supabase client and types
- `supabase/functions/`: Edge Functions for backend logic

## Performance Considerations

- Component lazy loading for code splitting
- React Query caching to minimize API calls
- Optimized re-renders with proper dependency arrays
- Efficient data structures for large employee lists

## Security Features

- Row Level Security (RLS) in Supabase
- Role-based access control
- Input validation and sanitization
- Secure environment variable handling
- JWT-based authentication
